summary: Conjecture 1.2 posits that the optimal exponent for homeomorphs of any fixed 3-graph $H$ is $5/2$; the source paper itself establishes the weaker universal bound $3-1/5 \approx 2.8$. Maya Sankar (2024) subsequently improved this upper bound to $8/3 \approx 2.667$ for all 2-dimensional simplicial complexes, bringing it closer to the conjectured optimum. The full conjecture remains open.

since_posted.title: An Improved Turán Exponent for 2-Complexes
since_posted.claim: Proves that any $n$-vertex 2-dimensional simplicial complex with at least $C_S n^{8/3}$ facets contains a homeomorphic copy of any fixed 2-complex $S$, improving the universal exponent from $3-1/5$ toward the conjectured optimal $5/2$.

search_queries: 3-graph homeomorph universal exponent 5/2 Keevash Long Narayanan Scott 2004.02657
hypergraph homeomorph threshold n^{5/2} Brown-Erdos-Sos conjecture 3-uniform
notes: The Long-Narayanan-Yap paper (Discrete Analysis, ~2020) extends the universal exponent framework to all dimensions k with exponent $\lambda_k \geq k^{-2k^2}$, but does not improve the 2-dimensional case over the source paper. Sankar (2024) is the primary improvement for 2-complexes specifically. The conjecture that $5/2$ is the correct universal exponent for 3-graph homeomorphs remains open.
